title: The First Cycle — Concordia Compass
description: You've cooked, eaten, fought, and communed. The hub is ready to show you four more loops. Concordia herself walks you through: the command palette, NPC contextual actions, building workbenches, and the run-mode hotbar.

objectives.description: Press Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) to open the command palette.
Click on any NPC to open the seven-action context menu.
Walk within 4 metres of a workbench-type building. The overlay opens automatically.
Launch a run-mode from the bottom hotbar (roguelite, horde, extraction, time loop, horror, or brawl).

breadcrumbs.content: Ctrl+K is your second-most-used keybinding (after WASD). It searches 240 lenses, every named action in the world, and every NPC by name. Use it.

Every NPC offers more than dialogue. Mentorship, courtship, combat invites, trade, hire, trait inspection. The menu is contextual to what the NPC offers, so a vendor shows Trade and a mentor shows Request Mentorship.

Eleven building types open workbenches by proximity: farm_plot, restaurant, trivia_kiosk, karaoke_booth, mahjong_table, hacking_terminal, programming_console, factory_workbench, attraction_booth, creature_pen, glyph_altar, mystery_board. Walk within 4m. The overlay opens.

Six modes from the hotbar: roguelite (Hades-pattern), horde wave (bullet-heaven), extraction run (Tarkov-pattern), time loop (Outer Wilds-pattern), asymmetric horror (Phasmophobia-pattern), brawl matchmaker (Sifu fist-only 1v1). Try each in any order.
